mechanism_narrative: A rising level of industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings higher and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls for 3 consecutive months but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than half of those months, this hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, it signals labor market slack and falling consumer spending, leading to negative corporate profits and downward pressure on equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE crosses above 6% but SPX 60-day return is positive in more than 40% of such events, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales and accommodative monetary policy combine to boost consumer spending and reduce discount rates, amplifying positive equity returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth > 3% and FEDFUNDS below 4%
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but SPX 5-day return is negative more often than positive, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, resulting in negative forward returns over the long horizon.
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals weakness in the housing sector and broader economic contraction, dragging down equities over the next week.
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1200 but SPX 5-day return is positive in more than half of cases, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high unemployment and rising inflation (stagflation) creates a toxic environment for equities as margins compress and policy options are limited, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: If both conditions persist but SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 40% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in consumer price inflation forces the Fed to tighten policy, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL inflation above 3% year-over-year is associated with positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market with strong wage growth and consumer confidence, supporting corporate earnings and equity prices over the near term.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines to low levels (below 5%) but SPX 20-day returns are negative in a majority of observations, reject.
